2605.09285.title: BetaEdit: Null-Space Constrained Sequential Model Editing
2605.09285.paper_summary: Null-space-based methods have garnered considerable attention in model editing by constraining updates to the null space of the pre-existing knowledge representation, thereby preserving the model's original behavior. However, in practice these methods rely on an approximate null space--leading to knowledge leakage--and further suffer from severe performance degradation during sequential editing. Recent work shows that history-aware editing strategies can empirically mitigate this decline, yet the underlying reason remains unclear. In this paper, we first expose the knowledge leakage inherent in existing null-space approaches and then analyze why history-aware updates effectively preserve both editing performance and general capabilities during long-horizon editing. Building on these insights, we propose BetaEdit, a refined framework that effectively controls the knowledge leakage and integrates history-aware updates into the null-space paradigm. Extensive experiments on three large language models across two standard benchmarks show that BetaEdit consistently outperforms prior methods in the challenging regime of massive-scale sequential editing. Code is available at: https://github.com/lbq8942/BetaEdit.
2605.09285.paper_summary_zh: 基於零空間的方法在模型編輯中引起了相當大的關注，因為它們將更新限制在現有知識表示的零空間內，從而保留了模型的原始行為。<lb/>然而，在實踐中這些方法依賴於近似的零空間——導致知識洩漏——並且在序列編輯過程中進一步遭受嚴重的性能下降。<lb/>最近的研究顯示，歷史感知編輯策略可以在實證上減輕這一衰退，但其根本原因仍然不清楚。<lb/>在本文中，我們首先揭示了現有零空間方法中固有的知識洩漏，然後分析了為什麼歷史感知更新能有效保護長期編輯中的編輯性能和一般能力。<lb/>基於這些見解，我們提出了BetaEdit，一個精煉的框架，有效控制知識洩漏並將歷史感知更新整合到零空間範式中。<lb/>在兩個標準基準上的三個大型語言模型上進行的廣泛實驗表明，BetaEdit在大規模序列編輯的挑戰性範疇中始終優於先前的方法。<lb/>代碼可在以下位置獲得：https://github.com/lbq8942/BetaEdit。
2605.09285.author: Bingqing Liu et.al.
2605.09285.authors: Bingqing Liu, Wei Liu, Yuhua Li
